compiled_state.title: As a developer, ticket show runs via a sourceable bash library function in under 0.15s on Linux
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Create `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh` — a sourceable bash library that exposes ticket operations as functions. Implement the first function, `ticket_show`, and wire the CLI dispatcher to source the library and delegate `ticket show` invocations to the function instead of exec'ing `ticket-show.sh`. This is the walking skeleton — it proves the sourceable-library + thin-dispatcher pattern works end-to-end and hits the SC2 read target.

**Why**: The sourceable library is the mechanism that eliminates the 3-process bash chain for every ticket operation. Implementing the full library in one step would be too large; building the skeleton first with one representative op (`ticket_show`, the most-called op across all DSO skills) validates the architecture before committing the other ops (Story D). The architectural shift from exec-style to source-style dispatch is significant and must be handled carefully so side effects do not leak into the dispatcher's shell.

**Scope**:
- IN: New file `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh`; sourceability contract enforced from day one (no `set -euo pipefail` at library scope, no `exit` calls from library functions — use `return`; no `trap EXIT` at library scope — use subshell `( ... )` for any local trap needs; no mutation of `GIT_DIR`/`GIT_INDEX_FILE`/`GIT_WORK_TREE`/`GIT_COMMON_DIR`; function namespace uses `_ticketlib_`-prefix convention or equivalent to avoid caller shadowing); `_ticketlib_dispatch <op> <args...>` helper that wraps each sourced call in a subshell to isolate library-function side effects from the dispatcher; `ticket_show` function that replaces the current `ticket-show.sh` logic; CLI dispatcher modified for `ticket show` to source the library and invoke the function (uncovered subcommands continue to exec their `.sh` script unchanged until Story D lands).
- OUT: Other library functions (Story D); `write_commit_event` rehome (Story C); sourceability regression test suite (Story E); platform-matrix testing (Story F); extension pattern documentation (Story D).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh` exists as a sourceable library file and defines `ticket_show`
  ← Satisfies: "The CLI dispatcher sources the library and delegates all covered operations to library functions"
- When this story is complete, the CLI dispatcher for `ticket show <id>` sources the library and calls the function instead of exec'ing `ticket-show.sh` — verified by a test that confirms no subshell-exec'd `ticket-show.sh` process is observed during a `ticket show` call
  ← Satisfies: "The CLI dispatcher sources the library and delegates all covered operations to library functions"
- When this story is complete, `ticket show` completes in <0.15s per call on Linux reference hardware measured via hyperfine over 10 warm runs (vs the A0 baseline)
  ← Satisfies: "Read-path operations (ticket show, list) complete in <0.15s per call"
- When this story is complete, sourcing `ticket-lib-api.sh` does NOT cause a caller's `set -euo pipefail` to abort on library code, does NOT install any persistent `trap EXIT` on the caller, does NOT mutate `GIT_*` env vars visible to the caller, and does NOT define function names that would shadow common caller-side function names — verified by a fixture test that sources the library in a strict-mode caller shell and asserts a clean post-source state
  ← Satisfies: "Sourcing the library is side-effect-free to the caller's shell"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Library design MUST expose a conventional extension pattern so downstream epics (2c28-e5b9, 4931-f639) can add subcommands with minimal per-function LOC — the formal pattern spec is written in Story D
- [Testing] Dispatcher architectural shift from exec-style to source-style means the dispatcher's existing `set -euo pipefail` now applies inside the library; the `_ticketlib_dispatch` helper's subshell wrap is the isolation mechanism

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
